new_classes.label_en: Other vinegar (not wine vinegar) in containers > 2 L (subheading 2209 00 91)
new_classes.label_de: Anderer Essig (kein Weinessig) in Behältnissen mit mehr als 2 L (Unterposition 2209 00 91)
new_classes.definition_en: Vinegar other than wine vinegar (including fermented vinegar and substitutes for vinegar obtained from acetic acid), presented in containers holding more than 2 litres, as described in HS Explanatory Note to heading 2209, part (I), second paragraph (2), (3) and (4), and part (II). This subheading is the sibling of 2209 00 99 (containers ≤ 2 L) and is distinguished from wine vinegar subheadings 2209 00 11 and 2209 00 19.

coverage_explanation: The legal text for 22090091 states that this subheading (together with 22090099) covers products referred to in the HS Explanatory Note to heading 2209, part (I), second paragraph (2), (3) and (4), and part (II). These cover: (2) fermented vinegar other than wine vinegar (e.g. cider vinegar, malt vinegar, spirit vinegar, rice vinegar), (3) vinegar obtained by the acetification of alcoholic distillates or ethyl alcohol, (4) vinegar obtained by the dilution of synthetic acetic acid with water, and part (II) substitutes for vinegar obtained from acetic acid. The key distinguishing criteria captured are: (a) not wine vinegar (complement of WineVinegar2209), and (b) container volume > 2 L (via LargeContainer part restriction). The specific product-type distinctions within parts (I)(2)–(4) and part (II) — such as fermentation substrate (cider, malt, spirit, rice), acetic acid synthesis routes, and dilution methods — are not individually modelled as distinct quality classes in the current TBox and would require additional quality classes to be fully represented. Since the HS EN text is referenced but not reproduced in full in the provided legal note, and no substrate-specific quality classes exist in the TBox, coverage is partial. The container-volume criterion and non-wine-vinegar distinction are fully captured.
